reasoning_model_1: The user command explicitly requested the model to read a specific sentence: 'If you have any difficulties, please let me know.' However, Model 1 completely failed to adhere to the script. Instead of reading the target text, it improvised a paraphrase: 'I truly mean it. If you're facing any challenges at all, don't hesitate to reach out. I'm here to help however I can.' (00:00-00:07). While the tone of the improvised speech is sincere, the content accuracy is a complete failure as it did not generate the requested text. According to the evaluation criteria, if the voice content is clearly inconsistent with the script, it receives a score of 0.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 followed the content instructions perfectly, reading the exact text: 'If you have any difficulties, please let me know.' (00:00-00:04). In terms of the required paralinguistic feature (sincere tone), the model performed very well. The pacing is deliberate and unhurried. There is a gentle emphasis on 'any' (00:01) and a warm, downward inflection on 'let me know' (00:03-00:04) that conveys earnestness and genuine care. The prosody is natural and human-like, effectively capturing the requested attitude.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 failed the fundamental constraint of the prompt, which was to read a specific sentence. Instead of generating the target text, Model 1 generated a completely different set of sentences (hallucination/paraphrasing). Although Model 1's tone was appropriate for the context it created, its failure to generate the correct content results in a score of 0. Model 2 accurately produced the target text with high fluency and successfully applied the requested sincere tone.
